title: The One With The Home Study

lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock and Wendy Knoller
Final check by Kim
Central Perk
Joey and Ross enter. Phoebe and Mike are sitting on the couch, reading a magazine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey you guys!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh, figuring out our wedding plans.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's funny, we were doing the same thing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's really crazy! The hall, the dress, the food... I-I had no idea how expensive this stuff was!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah it is really pricey. I mean, I freaked when I first heard the numbers.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So what did you two do about it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It was pretty simple actually, I came up with a couple of cost-cutting solutions, wrote out a list and Monica told me to go to hell.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There's no way around it Pheebs, you just gonna have to accept the fact that this is gonna cost you a lot of money.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I heard that weddings are like a 40 billion dollar a year industry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, and I'm responsible for just like half of that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But really, it does seem like this money could be put to better use?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Now, how would you feel if we gave all the wedding money to charity and we just got married at City Hall?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I think it would make me wanna marry you even more. (he kisses her)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I've got to say you guys, that's an incredible gesture!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross) Maybe you do that next time you get married!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, no. The next time it's gonna be a Hawaii at sunset. [pause] But maybe the time after that!
Opening Credits
Monica and Chandler's apartment
Monica is cleaning the table, Chandler is sitting on the sofa. Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Our adoption social worker is coming by today so we are cleaning the apartment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sarcastically) We?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know you don't want me to help. You can't have it both ways!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, is this person who decides whether or not you... get a baby?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Kind of. She's coming by to interview us and see where we live.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And it has to go perfectly, because if she doesn't like something about us she can keep us off every adoption list in the state.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, maybe I should stop by! She could be a soap opera fan! It's very impressive when the little people know a celebrity.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (pointing at himself) Little people?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pointing at himself) Celebrity.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, so I think I'm just about done here, unless you have any bad stuff hidden somewhere, like... porn or cigarettes?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What...? NO!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (he stands up and he feels very offended) I don't, and I'm offended by the insinuation!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, so there's not a magazine under the couch, or a pack of cigarettes taped to the back of the toilet tank, or a filthy video in the VCR?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll admit to the cigarettes and the magazine, but that tape is not mine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It isn't mine!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (going out with the VCR in his hand) Well, I guess we'll never know whose it is!
The New York City Children's fund hallway.

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes. We're here to make a rather sizeable donation to the children.

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Well, any contribution, large or small, is always appreciated.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I think you're gonna appreciate it the crap out of this one (she gives him a check)

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Well, this is very generous!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And we don't want any recognition. This is completely anonymous.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Completely anonymous. From two kind strangers.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mr. X and Phoebe Buffay.

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Well if you like, we can include your names in our newsletter.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Not necessary.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Buffay is spelled B-U-F-F-A-Y.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: And "X" is spelled uhm... "Mike Hannigan".

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Right. Well, on behalf of the children: thank you both very much.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure, I so glad we did this. It feels so good!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It does. It feels really good!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, look! And we get these free t-shirts! (she takes a t-shirt which was on the counter)

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Oh, actually, that's the shirt I wore to the gym.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mhm... it's moist.
Joey and Rachel's apartment.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he enters) Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Emma will be up in a minute!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, good!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh hey Ross... Listen, I heard about you and Charlie. I'm really sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, that's OK. I'm sure there are tons of other beautiful paleontologists out there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There was one! She's it! All the rest look like they should live under a bridge!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, uhm... what are you gonna do today?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I was thinking of taking Emma to the playground!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, what!?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Like I said I was thinking of taking Emma to the museum of knives and fire!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, look, Ross. I do not want Emma going to the playground.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Be-caaauuuse...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (upset) All right, well, if you must know... I had a traumatic... swing incident... when I was little.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Seriously?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, I was 4 years old and I was on the swing and then all of a sudden my hair got tangled in the chain. And to get me out my mom had to-had to cut a big chunk of my hair! (crying) And it was uneven for weeks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastic) And you made it through that? I wonder who's gonna play you in the movie!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, fine! You can make fun of me. I do not want Emma going there. And I was thinking Claire Danes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I'm sorry to hear about your tragedy, ok? But the swings are perfectly safe, and besides Emma loves them. You know what, you should come with us and you'll see!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, those things go like 40 miles an hour! Ok? When you're... and there is that moment when you are at the top, when you just don't know if you're gonna return back to earth!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Space is filled with orbiting children. (pause) Look, please, just come on, you know, when you'll see the look on Emma's face, I swear you won't regret it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good, you don't want to be one of those mothers who pass on their irrational fears on their children, do you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Irrational, huh? All right, well, I'll remember that the next time you freak out about a spider in your apartment!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah, that's the same, I am sure there are thirty different species of poisonous swings!
Monica's apartment. Somebody knocks the door

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, the adoption lady is early!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, ok, here we go.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here we go. Stand up straight. (smiling) Big smile. (opens the door and both are smiling exaggeratedly)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello, is this the creepy residence?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're waiting for the adoption lady, but, hey, I'm glad you're here. I was cleaning this morning and I found this (she puts a box on the table and opens it). I don't know if you wanna use it, but...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Awe, this is so sweet of you! But you know what? I won't be needing a veil, I actually won't be wearing a dress at all!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I told you! I am not coming to a naked wedding!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no, we're not having a big reception, we took the money we were gonna spend on a wedding and we donate them to the children charity.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's crazy! (Phoebe looks bewildered). I am sorry. I just can't imagine giving up my one wedding day like that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We, you know, we're different! We don't care about having a huge party. (She picks up the veil) This is really nice for you, but, oh, please, I put this on? (she puts it on) And, ow, I look (she looks her reflex image on a toaster), why, well, radiant. (pause) All right, well, who cares, I don't need a pretty veil and a fancy dress.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right. You're making a commitment and that's the same, whether you do that at the Plaza or, where are you gonna do it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: City Hall.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ow! (Chandler slaps her on her back) Oh, that sounds nice! I am just there for jury duty. They really spruce that place up!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's ok, it's ok. I made my decision. What I really want is a great big wedding (she covers her mouth)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But you already gave all your money to charity!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'll just ask for it back!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't think you can do that!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not! This is her wedding day, this is way more important than some stupid kids!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's sweet, honey, but save something for the adoption Lady.
The playground. Ross put Emma on the swing and they're ready to play

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, careful.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Careful, watch her hair. WATCH HER HAIR!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, she's got like three hairs!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know (she touches Emma's head) but they're just so beautiful! Oh, my God, I just pulled one out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I promise you she's safe! No watch how much she loves this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok.
Ross (to Emma): Ready sweety?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here we go! (He starts pushing Emma)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, careful, ok. (Emma giggles) Oh, she's smiling! Oh my God, she does like it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: See, I told you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Awe! (Emma laughs) Oh my God! Looks, she's a little dare-devil! Oh, let me push, can I push?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, absolutely!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok. Oh God. (To Ross) Get the camera, it's in the diaper bag.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok! (he takes the camera and walks backwards to take a shot) See? Scared of swings, I bet you feel pretty silly (a swinging boy knocks him down) Ow!
The New York City Children's fund

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: We're seriously asking for our money back?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's for our wedding day! Right, now, is this guy gay or straight, because one of us gonna have to start flirting.

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Wow! Are you here to make another donation the same day? I don't think that that's ever happened before.
Phoebe (to Mike): Gay, go.
Mike (to the charity guy): Oh my God, I love your shirt!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The donation we made earlier, we k..., we w..., we want it back.

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. See, that money was for a big wedding, that we thought we didn't want, but it turns out we do.

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: So you're asking us to refund your donation to the children?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah! This feels really good.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I am sorry. I am, but this wedding is just really important to me.

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Hey, it's not my business, (he takes their check from a drawer) besides it's probably a good thing. We really would have been spoiling the children, all those food, and warm clothing...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, that's not fair! A person's wedding is important! And especially to me! Ok? I didn't have a graduation party! And I didn't go to Prom. And I spent my sweet sixteen being chased round a tire yard by an escaped mental patient who is his own words wanted to "kill me" or whatever. So I deserve a real celebration and I am not gonna let some sweaty little man make me feel badly about it.( She storms out)

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: She could have been talking about either one of us.
Monica's apartment. Somebody knocks the door

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Hi, I am Laura, I am here for your adoption interview.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, I am Monica and this is Chandler. Please come in.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Would you like something to drink?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Oh, water would be fine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok. Great. I am so glad that you are here. We're really excited about getting this process started.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, because we love kids. Love 'em to death.Well, not actually to death, that's just a figure of speech - we love kids the appropriate amount... as allowed by law.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Your place is just lovely.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah, thank you. This building does have a wholesome family feel to it.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: You know, I... I feel like I've been here before. Are any other couples in the building adopting?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that that couple on the first floor? Because we should get a baby before them. Yeah! That guy tried to sell me drugs. (Laura looks shocked)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But other than that... wholesome, wholesome building.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: I just realized why I remember this place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really? What is it?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Oh, it's nothing. I went on a date with a guy who lived in this building and it didn't end very well.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh... that wouldn't by any chance be... Joey Tribbiani?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Of course it was!

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Yeah, we had a really great night and in the morning he promised he would call me and he didn't.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: RAT BASTARD!

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: So you're not friends with him?

lines.speaker: Monica and Chandler
lines.text: OH GOD NO! Nope, no, no, no. No! No, no. Nope! No, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no. NO! (finally Monica concludes) No!

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Well, I'm sorry I brought it up. So, are either one of you planning on staying at home with your child... (someone knocks on the door)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (form the other side of the door) Hellooo? Anybody in there order a celebrity? (He starts to enter the apartment and Chandler runs to the door and shuts it back in his face) OW!

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, it's just some crazy guy who roams the halls here. He's great with kids though.
Ross and Rachel are at the playground with Emma. Rachel in putting her back in the stroller and Ross is tending to his wound

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, oh Ross, oh my God, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: SON OF A BITCH! (turns to his right to see three kids staring at him) (To the kids) Oh relax! I didn't say the 'F' word! (They go away)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, see! I told you, those swings are evil! Alright, that is it. That is the last time Emma is getting on one of those things for her entire life.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No, no, no, no, okay, it wasn't the swing's fault. It was my fault and kind of that (point to the kid that kicked him) kids fault. Who is still laughing. Nice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, c'mon, please. Can we just get out of here, before somebody else gets hurt?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No wait, okay, okay, I have an idea. I want you to get on the swing, okay? And you'll see that there's nothing to be afraid of.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looks at him suspiciously) I know what this is all about... You've always been jealous of my hair.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I just think you're an adult, okay? And you should get over your silly fears.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Alright fine. I'll do it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: If you hold a spider.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (He freaks out and starts jumping around brushing his sweater) WHAT? WHERE? WHERE?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: IF you hold a spider.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. (Rachel bends down to Emma and Ross looks over his shoulder again, afraid)
Chandler and Monica's apartment. Chandler is still leaning against the door, keeping Joey out, who is still banging and shouting on the other side.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Guys? Everything ok? It's me, Joe...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Screams to interrupt Joey) AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA......AAAaaa-doption!!

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, just like I said. That crazy... Bert... roaming the halls. (Joey bangs on the door again)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Guys!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Keep on roaming Bert! We don't want any crazy today!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: WE'LL TALK TO YOU LATER, BERT. EVERYTHNG'S FINE!!
cut to Joey on the other side, who finally leaves the door and goes to his apartment

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Everything doesn't sound fine!

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Is he alright out there by himself?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah! He has a caretaker. His older brother... Ernie.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Bert and Ernie!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (nervous smile) You can't make this stuff up!
Central Perk. Phoebe and Mike enter

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You never told me about that guy on your sweet sixteen. Oh, ugh. I'm sorry about that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Lightly) Oh! It ended okay. One of my friends shot him.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, hey, at least you're getting a proper wedding. I mean, you really deserve that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I really do. You know, I had nothing growing up. (thinks for a few seconds) Just like the kids I took the money from.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No! No, no. I see where this is going. Don't make me go back there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look, I can't have a wedding with this money now. It's tainted.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Alright, fine. We'll give the money back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And if that guy at the charity gives us a hard time, my friend hasn't shot anyone in a really long time.
[Scene: Monica and Chandler's. They are preparing to show Laura around. Laura is standing with her back to the window, Chandler and Monica are standing on either side of her, facing each other.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Well, I must say, this seems like a lovely environment to raise a child in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, by the way, you are more than welcome to look under any of the furniture, because, believe me, you won't find any porn or cigarettes under there!

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Oh! Well, actually, before we look around, let me make sure I have everything I need up to here...

She starts checking her form. Chandler sees movement near the window from the corner of his eye and when he looks he spots Joey climbing up the fire escape and onto their balcony. He warns Monica silently.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Pulls Laura into the spare room) Why don't I show you the baby's room?
Joey enters through the side window and jogs towards the kitchen holding a baseball bat

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What the hell are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, you wouldn't let me in, so I thought you were in trouble.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we're not.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But you called me 'Bert'!? That's our code word for danger!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We don't have a code word.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We don't? We really should. From now on, 'Bert' will be our code word for danger.
Monica talks loudly in the baby's room

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So that was the baby's room. (They come out and Chandler throws Joey behind the couch and puts his foot on him. Monica looks at Chandler)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Chandler) What room should we see next?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Any room that isn't behind this couch! (laughs nervously)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs nervously as well, Laura looks confused) (To Laura) Some people don't get him, but I think he's really funny! (She takes Laura to their own bedroom).
Joey gets up and look annoyed

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (quivering with anger) I did not care for that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (escorting Joey to the door) You have to get out of here. You slept with our social worker and you never called her back and she is still pissed, so she can't see you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, ok! (He leaves)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok!
Joey leaves and closes the door behind him. Chandler walks towards the living room, but then Joey enters again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I forgot my bat.
He picks up his bat and holds it up, but then Monica and Laura enter the living room again. When Laura sees Joey, she freezes...

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And for the last time, we do not want to be friends with you! And we don't want to buy your bat! (Joey lowers his bat)

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Chandler) Bert! Bert! Bert! Bert!

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Are you friends with him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can explain... Joey...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhm... ok... uhm... Well, yeah... You have got some nerve, coming back here. I can't believe you never called me.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh... yeah... Probably you don't even remember my name. It's Joey, by the way. And don't bother telling me yours, because I totally remember it... lady. Yeah! I waited weeks for you to call me.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: I gave you my number, you never called me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no! Don't try to turn this around on me, ok? I'm not some kind of... social work, ok, that you can just... do.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: (embarrassed towards Chandler and Monica) Well, I'm pretty sure I gave you my number.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really? Think about it. Come on! You're a beautiful woman, smart, funny, we had a really good time, huh? If I had your number, why wouldn't I call you?

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: I don't know... Well, maybe I'm wrong... I'm sorry...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, hey, no! Too late for apologies... ok? You broke my heart. You know how many women I had to sleep with to get over you? (and he leaves the apartment, leaving her shocked)

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Joey, wait!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (acting sad) NO! I waited a long time, I can't wait anymore... (and closes the door behind him)

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: (laughing nervously) I'm sorry that you had to see that. I'm so embarrassed...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that's really ok.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, that we totally understand. Dating is hard.

lines.speaker: Laura
lines.text: Boy, you people are nice... And I've got to say... I think you're going to make excellent parents.
Chandler and Monica hug each other, and then Joey enters the apartment again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: LAURA! (and points to her, very confident)
The New York City Children's Fund building. Phoebe and Mike are entering.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to the Charity guy) We're back!

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Are you here to take more money? Because, I think what you're looking for is an ATM.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No, no, we're here to give the money back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, because you know what, it's... it's all about the children.
the Charity guy smiles wanting to take the check, but Phoebe pulls it back again. His smile fades.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Although... it's also about the wedding... Ugh, alright... here. (she gives the check and pulls it back again) No... Oh God... Oh!

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: If I haven't said so already sir, (sarcastically pointing to Phoebe) congratulations!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: (takes the check from Phoebe) Ok, look! Enough! Alright? I'm stepping in. I'm putting my foot down! As your future husband I'm going to make this decision for us. (thinking) Now... what do you think we should do?

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: You know what? It's not your decision anymore.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: On behalf of the Children of New York, I reject your money.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But... but... but we're giving you this!

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Yeah... And I'm giving it back to you... Come on! Consider it a contribution. (gives the check to Phoebe)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looks at the amount on the check, and gasps) Well, this is very generous!

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Please, take the check, go have a great wedding and a wonderful life together.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well, I mean... It sounds good to me. And that way we can save up, come back in a few years and make an even bigger donation.

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: Absolutely! And when you do, make sure you ask for Brian.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, is that you?

lines.speaker: Charity guy
lines.text: No!
Chandler and Monica's apartment. They are sitting in their living room when the phone rings.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello...? Oh hi... Oh my God...! Really...? I can't wait to tell Chandler... Ok, goodbye. (hangs up)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wrong number?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was Laura... She gave us a great report and we are officially on the waiting list.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now we just have to wait for a call and... and someone tells us there's a baby waiting for us. Oh...
Chandler and Monica hug and after a while the telephone rings again... Monica's eyes get bigger. Chandler answers.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello...? Have you seen Joey's bat?
COMMERCIAL BREAK
The playground. Ross, Rachel and Emma are still there. Rachel comes walking to Ross and Emma with something between her hands.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok... I got a spider. There were two, I picked the bigger one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (nervously) Ok...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok... (and passes the spider to Ross who holds it in between his hands)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Gasps and speaks at a higher pitch) This feels perfectly normal. Ok, get on the swing!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (reluctantly) Ok... O-k...
She slowly grabs the chains of the swing, swings her hair back, and sits down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (more confident) Ok...
She slowly walks forward and backward, to gain speed...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: whoo... ok... wow... ok... OH!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: See?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A-alright! I can do this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There you go! Good for you! And you know what, I'm actually getting used to this little guy. I don't really even feel him in here anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's because he's on your neck.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well... (realizes, screaming like a little girl, trying to get rid of the spider) Whaa... aaah... aaahhh...

Ross is now preoccupied with the spider, and forgets that Rachel is still using the swing. While trying to get rid of the spider, he stands in front of Rachel, who bumps into him, throwing him on the ground again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ROSS!
Rachel tries to stop mid-swing, and the swing starts to turn from side to side
End
